_notes: WebShop HGPO-Progress (Method C) on the AlfWorld SOTA recipe (rank-32 + MLP target modules, K=8, kl=0.04, lr=5e-6, T=1.0 launcher) PLUS the new WebShop-specific dense signal (env.use_attribute_progress_intermediate_reward=true). Unlike flatGRPO which sets alpha=1.0 (decomposer mathematically inert; only trajectory-level advantage survives), Progress sets alpha=0.5 so the per-turn signal from the `progress` decomposer (which reads TurnRecord.raw_env_reward — i.e. the populated intermediate_reward field) ACTUALLY CONTRIBUTES to the per-turn advantage. lambda_consistency=0.1 matches the AlfWorld Progress reference (configs/method_hgpo_progress_alfworld.json). With the goal_options + user_sessions adapter fixes landed (verified via infra/app_webshop_sft_gen.py::validate_dense_signal: mean_ir on click_option = 0.0384, Pearson r(cum_IR, final_reward) = 0.9651), Progress is now a real per-turn-supervision baseline for WebShop — it's the parameter-free version of TurnRDv2 (TurnRD learns a model to attribute credit; Progress just uses the raw dense signal as the per-turn credit). seed=41 — disjoint from attention(11), flatGRPO(23), and LLMJudge(31). base_task_id_offset = 41×10×80 = 32800; train range [32800, 33600); eval range [6500, 6600) (disjoint). Launcher: scripts/run_webshop_SOTA_Progress_v1.sh. Plan: webshop_sft_mlpr32_oracle_baseline.
